examples.title: CFG to PDA Conversion Algorithm
examples.description: Converts Context-Free Grammar to Pushdown Automaton using empty stack acceptance
examples.explanation: The PDA starts with S on stack. For each non-terminal A on stack top, it can replace with any production α (reverse needed because stack is LIFO - leftmost symbol of α goes on top). For terminals, it matches and pops them from both input and stack. When stack becomes empty, the input string is accepted.

examples.title: CYK Membership Algorithm
examples.description: Determines whether a string can be generated by a CFG in CNF
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CYK(w[1..n], G in CNF)
BEGIN
 // n = length of input string
 // P[i,j] = set of non-terminals deriving w[i..j]
 
 // Initialize table
 FOR i = 1 TO n DO
 FOR A → a IN productions OF G DO
 IF w[i] == a THEN
 ADD A TO P[i,i]
 END IF
 END FOR
 END FOR
 
 // Fill table for longer substrings
 FOR length = 2 TO n DO
 FOR i = 1 TO n-length+1 DO
 j = i + length - 1
 // Try all split points
 FOR k = i TO j-1 DO
 FOR A → BC IN productions DO
 IF B IN P[i,k] AND C IN P[k+1,j] THEN
 ADD A TO P[i,j]
 END IF
 END FOR
 END FOR
 END FOR
 END FOR
 
 // Check if start symbol can derive entire string
 IF S IN P[1,n] THEN
 RETURN TRUE // w is in the language
 ELSE
 RETURN FALSE // w is not in the language
 END IF
END
examples.explanation: The algorithm uses dynamic programming. First, it fills diagonal with non-terminals that produce single terminals. Then for longer substrings, it checks all possible split points to see if any production can combine derivations of two parts. If start symbol S can derive the entire string, the string is accepted.

examples.title: Conversion to Chomsky Normal Form
examples.description: Algorithm to convert any CFG to CNF
examples.explanation: The conversion to CNF involves 5 major steps: removing ε-productions, removing unit productions, removing useless symbols, converting terminals in long RHS to non-terminals, and ensuring binary productions. Each step simplifies the grammar while preserving the language.
